channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Metro 2039 Story Leaks Ahead of Xbox First Look Reveal
item.content:encoded:
New details regarding Metro 2039 have emerged.

Metro 2039 is the fourth mainline title in the Metro series, and it is due to be revealed on April 16 in an Xbox First Look showcase. However, story details have emerged for the upcoming title, which was rumored to be following the storyline of the Metro 2034 book.

Details on Metro 2039’s Storyline

Courtesy of IGDB, a new synopsis for the game has been leaked. And what promises to be “the most shocking” title in the series yet, the synopsis teases a powerful campaign that weaves exploration, survival, combat, and stealth together.

The full synopsis can be seen below:

METRO 2039 is the next landmark FPS from acclaimed storytellers, 4A Games. Journey into the dark heart of post-apocalyptic Moscow in a harrowing conflict for the very soul of the Metro.

The year is 2039. For a quarter century, the last survivors of nuclear war have been locked in bitter conflict deep in the Metro tunnels beneath the poisoned city of Moscow. Now, finally, they have been united by a single leader, a fanatical Spartan known as Hunter.

But this is no peace: Hunter’s Novoreich regime rules through propaganda and fear, subjugating the population in the name of a new war for the surface against a dark and terrifying enemy.

In the wilderness beyond the city, the reclusive Stranger is haunted by violent waking nightmares. When the ghosts of his past force him out of exile, he must undertake a harrowing journey into the shattered ruins of Moscow, to the one place he swore he’d never go. The Metro.

Experience the most shocking METRO adventure yet in a powerful single-player story that weaves exploration, survival, combat, and stealth across a hauntingly beautiful but deadly world with unparalleled immersion.

Inspired by the worldwide best-selling METRO book trilogy by Dmitry Glukhovsky.

Insider Gaming will have full coverage of the event on April 16. Learn more about the upcoming showcase here.

Are you excited for Metro 2039? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Embark Unveils New Images of Next ARC Raiders Map
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios has started drip-feeding content teasing the next ARC Raiders map, coming as part of the Riven Tides update at the end of April. It’s supposed to be the biggest and most important update since ARC Raiders came out in October 2025, and it’s bringing with it a new map that looks to be centered around a massive hotel complex.

Today’s tease came in the form of an email, sent to random gamers and talking about the ‘Panorama Azzurro’, a sprawling, derelict hotel. It also provides some snapshot images of the hotel, revealing a location that’s literally crumbling.

Riven Tides Map Revealed Further by Embark

Embark Studios is slowly uncovering more of the new Riven Tides update, set to arrive in a couple of weeks. It’ll expand ARC Raiders immeasurably, adding the game’s first new map in five months, introducing a large-scale ARC enemy, and bringing in a new Raider Deck, Project, and fresh quests.

In an email doing the rounds today, Embark Studios unveiled more information about Riven Tides with ‘Field Observation 001’, which suggests more intel is incoming.

Here’s what the email says:

Time: 18:06

Location: Hotel front/interior

Grid Ref: Marked

Reporting Scout: ********

Panorama Azzurro.

Shani was right, the old resort is still standing.

You can still find the disco posters on the walls.

I want to do a full sweep, but I’m not sure the stairs won’t crumble underneath me.

Action: Marking the coordinates and pulling back to the tennis courts.

Status: Standby

Here are the images associated with the email:

From what we know, Riven Tides will introduce a coastal map, and that’s backed up by this being a resort. We’ve also seen changes to the in-game map that showcase something existing at a broken portion of the game’s coastline. There are references to a hotel, a cargo port, and beaches.

This could be a major enough update to introduce an ARC enemy tied to the ocean. Players have long since become bored by the flying ARC enemies that are so commonplace in ARC Raiders now, and they want something truly fresh.

What do you think we’re getting in Riven Tides this month in ARC Raiders? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Starfield’s PS5 issues have been addressed

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Bethesda Addresses Starfield’s PS5 Performance Issues and Crashes
item.content:encoded:
In a post on social media, Bethesda Game Studios has addressed the slew of complaints from PS5 users experiencing rampant issues in Starfield. The game dropped last week, and since it arrived, players have been reporting crashes aplenty, taking to social media to vent their frustrations about the game they waited almost three years to receive.

While Bethesda didn’t go into any kind of granularity about the particular root causes of these issues, they did stress that they’re ‘aiming to release a fix’ this week.

Starfield PS5 Crashes Are Being Fixed

Bethesda Game Studios has formally addressed the issues raised recently by PlayStation 5 players diving into Starfield and hitting crashes left, right, and centre:

Thanks for sharing your adventures across the Settled Systems this weekend — we’ve loved reading them! We’re aware of some reported PS5 crashing issues and have narrowed them down to a small number of causes. We’re addressing these in a hotfix we’re aiming to release this week. Thanks for flagging, and we’ll keep you posted.

Starfield came to PS5 on April 7th after almost three years of waiting. It arrived alongside the massive Free Lanes update and the game’s second major expansion, dubbed Terran Armada. Unfortunately, Terran Armada did nothing to revitalize the game’s dwindling sentiments, but that’s a topic for another article.

The comments under the post above were mixed, with some claiming that they’ve had constant crashes on other platforms since the game came out, and Bethesda’s attempts to patch up the title have made little difference.

One user wrote a scathing comment:

Bethesda, what is actually going on at your offices because from what we are seeing you’re doing the absolute bare minimum when it comes to your games now.

Have you been experiencing issues with Starfield on PS5? Let us know how you’re coping on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Xbox’s CEO thinks Game Pass is too expensive

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Hastr0 Claims Overwatch Is Back as an Elite Esport
item.content:encoded:
Overwatch is becoming a major esport again, according to Envy/Dallas Fuel owner Mike ‘Hastr0’ Rufail.

The former pro Call of Duty player took to Twitter to talk about his excitement for the game’s resurgence in popularity and his belief that things are moving in the right direction.

There has been a growing excitement surrounding Overwatch after Blizzard removed the “2” from the name and returned the game to its 6v6 roots.

Along with the name change, Blizzard released five brand new heroes to the game all at once to refresh the hero shooter. And Overwatch is getting another new hero in Sierra soon.

Hast0 Proclaims Overwatch as a Tier 1 Esport Again

After the expensive failure that was the Overwatch League and the polarising release of Overwatch 2, interest in the game’s esports scene dropped significantly.

Yet, esports veteran Hastr0 claims that the esports scene is thriving again.

It likely helped that his Dallas Fuel team, which returned to Overwatch earlier this year, went undefeated and won the North American Overwatch Champions Series (OWCS) while he was tweeting about this.

Leading up to the team’s victory, Hastr0 shared how he was excited for Overwatch again:

“Overwatch is back as a Tier 1 esports title. That is undeniable at this point. The esports lootbox and drops are part of the resurgence alongside amazing advances by the dev team. Generational comeback.”

The lootboxes Hastro mentioned is part of the game’s new incentives for watching pro Overwatch matches.

Viewers receive in-game rewards for watching multiple hours of esports competitions, including one lootbox every for every 15 hours watched.

When asked why he thinks Overwatch esports are doing well again, Hastro responded:

“Major game updates from the dev team, incentive-based viewership rewards for watching the OWCS esports matches, the game is well-balanced and very fun to play right now in my opinion.”

Hastr0 admitted the rewards are a major factor and it certainly has worked as Overwatch esports viewership in 2026 has already overtaken the last couple of years.

According to Esports Charts, over 221.5k viewers tuned into the OWCS Pre-Season Bootcamp event earlier this year, beating Overwatch’s peak audiences in 2025 (177k) and 2024 (159k).

Though the hero shooter still has a long way to go to beat the game’s all time peak audience of just over 437k from the inaugural season of the Overwatch League.

Do you think Overwatch is becoming a major esport again? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

The esports scene is continuing to shift and evolve with ESL introducing big co-streaming rule changes, which could have widespread effects on the industry.
